data.title: Boeing's stock slides on reports of Chinese limits on U.S. aviation giant
data.description:
Boeing’s (NYSE:BA) shares on Tuesday fell 2.4% following multiple news reports that Chinese regulators told domestic airlines to halt new orders of planes from the U.S. aviation giant and to obtain official clearance before accepting delivery of previously purchased jets.

The move is another hurdle for Boeing (NYSE:BA) as it navigates escalating trade friction and economic headwinds. The company has long benefited from tariff-free access to key global markets, but is now facing mounting pressure from retaliatory trade measures.

The end of its decades-long duty-free treatment, coupled with broader global economic uncertainty, is creating serious challenges for the company’s extensive and vulnerable supply chain. U.S. tariffs and retaliatory measures by other countries are also dampening demand for air travel, compounding Boeing’s (BA) difficulties.

The company, which reported $67 billion in revenue last year, is already under financial strain. After burning through $14 billion in 2024, Boeing (BA) had aimed to return to positive cash flow by the end of this year, a goal now clouded by geopolitical and operational uncertainty.

Airbus rivalry

Analysts have warned that the evolving trade environment may benefit Boeing’s (BA) chief rival, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY), which already has a stronger foothold in China and operates final-assembly lines there.

While some existing deliveries to China could still proceed, airlines may choose to delay accepting aircraft to avoid high import tariffs. Boeing (BA) could take a financial hit of approximately $1.2 billion this year if all deliveries to China are halted, according to estimates from equity research firm Bernstein.

So far in 2025, Boeing (BA) has delivered 130 jets worldwide, 18 of which went to Chinese carriers.

Jetliners are among the most expensive items airlines purchase, with the 737 Max carrying a list price of more than $100 million. In recent years, demand for new aircraft soared as the aviation industry rebounded from the pandemic, but that momentum is showing signs of slowing. Rising travel costs and concerns over the economy are prompting some U.S. travelers to cancel or delay plans.

Ryanair (RYAAY) Chief Executive Michael O’Leary, in a recent interview with the Financial Times, said the airline might postpone receipt of 25 Boeing (BA) jets originally scheduled for August, potentially pushing deliveries into next year.

Supply-chain worries

Boeing (BA) Chief Executive Kelly Ortberg has expressed concern about the broader implications of tariffs, not just the direct cost of duties on aircraft parts and materials, but also the impact on global export capabilities and the overall resilience of the company’s supply network.

Although Boeing's (BA) current backlog exceeds 5,500 aircraft — enough to keep production lines running for years — the longer-term implications of restricted access to China, which is projected to represent 20% of global aircraft demand, could be severe.

China's domestically produced Comac C919 is not yet a full competitor. The company delivered just 13 jets in 2024 and still relies heavily on U.S.-made components. However, it may gain market share if Chinese carriers shift further away from Boeing (BA) amid trade tensions.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) has outpaced Boeing (BA) in China for several years and may also benefit if tariffs continue to make U.S.-built aircraft less economically viable. Unlike Boeing (BA), which only operates a completion facility in China, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) assembles aircraft in the country, giving it a logistical and political advantage.

Boeing’s (BA) supplier base, already battered by pandemic-era disruptions and last year’s widely reported manufacturing flaws — including a mid-flight panel failure on an Alaska Airlines jet — could face additional strain.

Many suppliers are smaller, financially fragile firms contending with labor shortages and rising input costs. The breakdown of a long-standing trade agreement that once shielded aircraft manufacturing from global tariffs is now threatening to further destabilize the sector.

data.title: United beats FQ1 estimates but issues profit warning if economy stalls
data.description:
United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) shares are moving higher in Tuesday’s after-hours as growth in the carrier’s basic economy fueled a better-than-expected profit of $0.91 per share, swinging from a loss of $0.15 in the same quarter last year and beating Wall Street’s expectations by 17 cents.

Gains have been restrained, however, by the company’s warning that its profitability will be adversely impacted if the economy enters a recession.

“United Next is on track and we will continue to execute our multiyear plan that has allowed United to thrive in any demand environment. It has given us industry-leading margins in the good times, and we expect to expand our lead further in challenging economic times,” said CEO Scott Kirby.

For the fiscal first quarter, United’s (NASDAQ:UAL) capacity increased by 4.9% year-over-year, total revenue per available seat mile (“TRASM”) was up 0.5%, while costs per available seat mile (“CASM”) were down 3.4%. Excluding fuel, CASM was up 0.3%. Domestic revenue was down 3.9%, while international revenue improved 5.2%. Load factor for the quarter was down 90 basis points to 79.2%, led by a 340-basis point decline in domestic load factor. This all contributed to a 5.3% gain in total revenue to $13.2B, slightly less than expected.

Unlike competitors in the airline space, Delta (DAL) and Frontier (ULCC), who withdrew 2025 guidance, United (NASDAQ:UAL) provided their outlook for the year, but with an alternative outlook should economic growth stall.

For the fiscal second quarter, the carrier projects an adjusted profit of $3.25 to $4.25 per share, straddling the consensus estimate of $3.97 per share.

For FY25, however, United (UAL) issued two sets of guidance. In what the airline characterizes as a “stable environment,” United (UAL) expects to earn an adjusted profit of $11.50 to $13.50 per share, but a significantly lower profit of $7.00 to $9.00 per share in a “recessionary environment.” This compares to the Street’s estimate of $10.36 per share.

Shares are up 6.5% in late trading.

data.title: PennantPark Floating Rate JV completes $301M securitization
data.description:
PennantPark Floating Rate Capital (NYSE:PFLT) said on Tuesday its unconsolidated joint venture, PennantPark Senior Secured Loan Fund ("PSSL"), closed a four-year reinvestment period, 12-year final maturity $301M debt securitization in the form of a collateralized loan obligation.

The CLO achieved PennantPark Floating Rate's (NYSE:PFLT) lowest AAA pricing in the platform's history, said PFLT CEO Arthur Penn. "With the closing of CLO 12, PennantPark now manages approximately $4.0 billion in CLO middle market assets, and we look forward to continued growth with the support of our current and new investors," he said.

 The term debt securitization is expected to be ~100% funded at close.  Proceeds from the debt will be used to repay a portion of PSSL's $325M secured credit facility.

In addition, PSSL will act as retention holder in the transaction to retain exposure to the performance of the securitized assets, the company said.

data.title: United Airlines in charts: Unit cost excluding fuel continues to rise sequentially in Q1
Protara Therapeutics appoints Leonardo Nicacio as chief medical officer
data.description:
• Protara Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TARA) has appointed Leonardo Nicacio as the chief medical officer.
  • Nicacio most recently served as Head of Clinical Development and Global Medical Affairs at Stemline Therapeutics. Prior to joining Stemline, Nicacioserved in various capacities in Seagen.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Community Health Systems to sell ownership interest in Cedar Park Regional Medical Center for $460M
data.description:
• Community Health Systems (NYSE:CYH) on Tuesday said subsidiaries of the company have entered into an agreement to sell their collective 80% ownership interest in Cedar Park Regional Medical Center and certain ancillary businesses located in Cedar Park, Texas, to subsidiaries of Ascension Health for $460 million in cash.
  • CYH +12.85% after hours to $2.81.
  • Ascension Health currently holds a minority ownership interest in Cedar Park Regional Medical Center and will purchase the remaining interest through this transaction.
  • The transaction is expected to close late in the second quarter or early in the third quarter of 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Janover partners with crypto exchange Kraken for Solana delegation to JNVR validators
data.description:
Commercial property financing solutions provider Janover (NASDAQ:JNVR) announced on Tuesday a strategic partnership with the crypto exchange Kraken.

JNVR shares were trading +2.33% post market at $77.00.

Kraken plans to delegate a portion of its current and future Solana (SOL-USD) to Janover-operated validators.

Validator revenue generated from Kraken’s delegation is expected to help fund Janover's operations, and to enable the company build its SOL position over time.

Kraken has more than 4.5M Solana of delegation worth ~$500M.

The news comes after former Kraken team acquired a majority ownership in Janover. Kraken is stepping into the traditional finance world, and is preparing to go public next year.

data.title: Interactive Brokers Q1 profit falls short, revenue beats; declares four-for-one stock split
data.description:
Interactive Brokers Group (NASDAQ:IBKR) on Tuesday delivered Q1 2025 results that came in mixed as earnings trailed the average analyst estimate while revenue slightly surpassed expectations. Shares slid 3.5% in after-hours trading.

The company also announced a four-for-one forward split of its common stock. Each record stockholder as of the close of market on June 16, will receive three additional shares, to be distributed after the close of market on Tuesday, 17. Trading is expected to start on a split-adjusted basis at market open on Wednesday, 18.

Meanwhile, Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.88, missing the $1.93 consensus, fell from $2.03 in Q4 2024 and rose from $1.64 a year earlier.

GAAP net revenue of $1.43B, exceeding the $1.41B expected, advanced from $1.42B in the earlier quarter and $1.20B in Q1 2024.

Total daily average revenue trades (DARTs) jumped 13% Q/Q and 50% from a year ago to 3.52M.

Commission revenue climbed 36% from a year before to $514M (vs. $477M in Q4) on higher customer trading volumes. Customer trading volume in stocks, options and futures gained 47%, 25% and 16%, respectively.

Execution, clearing, and distribution fees rose 20% Y/Y to $121M (vs. $115M in Q4), driven by higher SEC fee rate, a new FINRA Consolidated Audit Trail fee initiated during the Q4, and higher customer trading volumes.

Net interest income totaled $770M for the quarter ended March 31, 2025, down from $807M in the three-month period ended Dec. 31, 2024, and $747M in the year-ago quarter.

The number of customer accounts on Interactive Brokers (NASDAQ:IBKR) of 3.62M in Q1 compared with 3.34M in the prior quarter and increased 32% Y/Y.

Conference call at 4:30 p.m. ET.
